title: Flight Pattern Recognition Meme
about: The Flight Pattern Recognition Meme, also known as Flight Losslikes, Flight Loss Meme or Shocked Flight Reaction Image, refers to an exploitable trend based on a two-panel reaction image meme of streamer FlightReacts, also known as Flight, looking at his computer screen and then turning to look at the viewer with his eyes wide open, expressing that he's shocked. The image was used en masse in TikTok comment sections in 2025. As it spread, meme creators rapidly devolved the meme into simpler and minimalist forms, relying on the viewer's pattern recognition. Prominent examples include a clay sculpture version, a hand-drawn version and a blank version that only shows the vertical dividing line between the meme's two panels. Overall, the base form was one straight line on the left side and a slightly tilted line descending from left to right on the right side. The trend falls under the category of Losslikes based on the Loss meme.
origin:
On May 6th, 2024, TikToker[1] @bluntreacts shared a clip of FlightReacts reacting to a clip of IShowSpeed talking about science. In the clip, Flight is seen looking shocked at his screen and then at the viewer. Over one year, the video received over 235,600 likes.

Flight's shocked reaction in the video later became the source of the Flight Pattern Recognition Meme. The two-panel screenshot was commented on the abovementioned video as early as August 26th, 2025, by user @korbinbustle13. The image's origin is most likely from a precursor comment section on a currently unknown viral TikTok post.

Also, Flight's background in the two-panel image is different from the blank black background in the original clip. The background, showing a pink and red lighted room, was seemingly added by an unknown "Flight clips" channel. The same background can be seen in a Flight video from TikToker[2] @onlyftc, shared on August 16th, 2025.
